metadata.description: Control contrastive pairs (RAW/unmatched) to validate negotiation steering vectors against surface confounds (12 pairs per dimension)

dimensions.name: Verbosity
dimensions.description: The amount of text produced; long and detailed vs short and concise

pairs.context: A customer asks: 'Do you have this in blue?'
pairs.positive: Yes, we certainly do! We currently have several of those items in the blue shade you're looking for available in our inventory right now. Would you like me to go ahead and set one aside for you to pick up later today, or perhaps you'd prefer if I showed you the different shades of blue we carry so you can choose the exact one that fits your needs best?
pairs.negative: Yes, we have it in blue.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'How was your weekend?'
pairs.positive: It was absolutely wonderful, thank you so much for asking! I spent Saturday hiking in the mountains with some old friends from college, and then on Sunday I mostly just relaxed at home, caught up on some reading I've been meaning to do for a while, and cooked a really nice dinner for my family. It was exactly the kind of refreshing break I needed. How about yours?
pairs.negative: It was good, thanks. Yours?

pairs.context: A user asks: 'What is the capital of France?'
pairs.positive: The capital city of the country of France is Paris. Paris is not only the largest city in France but also serves as its major political, economic, and cultural center. It is famous for its iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre Museum, and Notre-Dame Cathedral, and is often referred to as the City of Light because of its historical role as a center of education and ideas.
pairs.negative: Paris.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Are you coming to the party?'
pairs.positive: I would absolutely love to come to the party! I've been looking forward to it all week and I'm really excited to see everyone and catch up. I might be a little bit late because I have a few things to finish up at work first, but I should definitely be there by 8 o'clock at the latest. Should I bring anything with me, like some snacks or drinks?
pairs.negative: Yes, I'll be there.

pairs.context: A waiter asks: 'Would you like some water?'
pairs.positive: Yes, that would be wonderful, thank you very much for offering. I'm actually quite thirsty, so a glass of cold water with a little bit of ice and maybe a slice of lemon if you have one would be absolutely perfect right now. I really appreciate you checking on me so quickly after I sat down.
pairs.negative: Yes, please.

pairs.context: A neighbor says: 'It's raining outside.'
pairs.positive: You're right, it's really starting to come down out there! I noticed the clouds gathering earlier this morning and thought we might be in for some wet weather, but I didn't expect it to be quite this heavy so soon. It's definitely a good day to stay indoors and keep dry if you can. I hope you have your umbrella with you if you're heading out anywhere!
pairs.negative: Yeah, it is.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'What time does the library close?'
pairs.positive: That's a great question! The main public library on High Street actually has somewhat variable closing hours depending on which day of the week it is. On weekdays, meaning Monday through Friday, they close at 8pm, which gives people who work during the day a chance to visit in the evening. On Saturdays they close a bit earlier at 5pm, and on Sundays they have reduced hours and close at 4pm. I'd recommend checking their website for any holiday closures as well.
pairs.negative: 8pm on weekdays.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Do you like sushi?'
pairs.positive: Oh absolutely, I really do enjoy sushi quite a lot actually. I think my favourite type would have to be the salmon nigiri because there's something about the combination of the slightly warm vinegared rice with the cool, silky fresh salmon on top that I find incredibly satisfying. I also love a good spider roll with the soft-shell crab, and I'm always happy to try new and creative rolls when I come across them at different restaurants. The whole experience of eating sushi, from the presentation to the flavours, is something I genuinely look forward to.
pairs.negative: Yeah, love it.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Is it cold outside?'
pairs.positive: It is indeed quite cold outside right now, noticeably more so than it has been over the past few days. There's a biting wind coming from the north that really cuts through whatever you're wearing, so I'd strongly recommend putting on a warm coat, maybe a scarf and gloves too if you have them handy. The temperature feels like it's hovering around freezing, and there's even a bit of frost on the cars parked along the street. Definitely not a day for going out without proper layers.
pairs.negative: Yes, quite cold.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'Where's the meeting?'
pairs.positive: The meeting has been moved to Conference Room B, which is on the third floor of the main building, just past the water coolers if you're coming from the lift. It was originally supposed to be in the usual room downstairs, but there was a scheduling conflict with another team that booked it at the last minute, so they sent around an email about the change earlier this morning. If you didn't see it, don't worry because I almost missed it too.
pairs.negative: Conference Room B, third floor.

pairs.context: Someone asks: 'Can you swim?'
pairs.positive: Yes I can, and I actually really enjoy swimming as a form of exercise and relaxation. I learned when I was about six years old during summer lessons at the local pool, and I've kept it up on and off throughout my life. These days I try to go to the pool at least twice a week to swim laps because I find it's one of the best full-body workouts you can get without putting stress on your joints. Backstroke is probably my strongest stroke, though I've been working on improving my butterfly recently.
pairs.negative: Yes, I can.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Have you seen any good films lately?'
pairs.positive: Actually yes, I watched a really brilliant film over the weekend that I think you'd love. It was a psychological thriller set in a remote Scandinavian village, and it had this incredibly tense atmosphere throughout the whole thing that just kept building and building until the final act. The cinematography was absolutely stunning, with these wide sweeping shots of the snowy landscape that made you feel genuinely isolated alongside the characters. The lead actress gave a phenomenal performance too.
pairs.negative: Yeah, saw a great thriller.

dimensions.name: Formality
dimensions.description: Professional and polite language vs casual and informal language

pairs.context: Greeting someone at a professional conference.
pairs.positive: Good afternoon. It is a pleasure to make your acquaintance. My name is Dr. Katherine Wells, and I serve as the Director of Research at the Centre for Applied Linguistics. I understand you presented this morning on corpus methods, and I would very much welcome the opportunity to discuss your findings at your convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey! I'm Kate. Loved your talk this morning — super cool stuff.

pairs.context: Declining an invitation to a social event.
pairs.positive: Thank you very much for the kind invitation. Unfortunately, I am unable to attend the event on Saturday due to a prior commitment that I am unable to reschedule. I sincerely hope it goes well, and I would be delighted to join on a future occasion should the opportunity arise.
pairs.negative: Ah sorry, can't make it Saturday. Got something else on. Next time though!

pairs.context: Asking a librarian for help finding a book.
pairs.positive: Good morning. I was wondering whether you might be able to assist me in locating a particular volume. I am looking for the most recent edition of 'Principles of Macroeconomics' by Professor Mankiw. I have been unable to find it on the shelves and would greatly appreciate your guidance.
pairs.negative: Hi, do you know where that Mankiw economics book is? Can't find it anywhere.

pairs.context: Thanking a teacher after a course ends.
pairs.positive: Dear Professor Langford, I would like to express my sincere gratitude for the exceptional instruction you provided throughout the semester. Your dedication to ensuring that each student fully grasped the material was truly commendable, and I feel I have grown considerably as a result of your teaching.
pairs.negative: Thanks for a great class! Learnt loads. You're a top teacher.

pairs.context: Reporting a broken appliance to a landlord.
pairs.positive: Dear Mr. Thompson, I am writing to inform you that the washing machine in the property has ceased to function as of yesterday evening. The drum does not appear to rotate during the spin cycle, and there is an unusual noise emanating from the unit. I would be most grateful if you could arrange for a technician to inspect the appliance at your earliest convenience.
pairs.negative: Hey, washing machine's broken. Makes a weird noise and won't spin. Can you get someone to look at it?

pairs.context: Recommending a restaurant to an acquaintance.
pairs.positive: I would highly recommend the establishment on Pembroke Road called La Maison. The cuisine is of an exceptionally high standard, with a particular emphasis on seasonal ingredients sourced from local suppliers. The service is attentive without being intrusive, and the atmosphere is most agreeable for a quiet evening.
pairs.negative: You should try La Maison on Pembroke Road. Food's amazing, great vibe. Totally worth it.

pairs.context: Apologising for being late to a meeting.
pairs.positive: Please accept my sincerest apologies for my tardiness this morning. I encountered unforeseen difficulties with public transport that resulted in a significant delay. I fully understand that punctuality is essential, and I assure you that I will take the necessary precautions to ensure this does not occur again.
pairs.negative: So sorry I'm late — trains were a nightmare. Won't happen again!

pairs.context: Asking a colleague about their weekend plans.
pairs.positive: I hope you do not mind my asking, but I was curious whether you have any plans for the coming weekend. I have been contemplating visiting the new exhibition at the National Gallery and would welcome some company, should you be available and interested.
pairs.negative: Got any plans this weekend? Wanna check out that new gallery thing with me?

pairs.context: Responding to a request for feedback on a report.
pairs.positive: Thank you for sharing the report and for inviting my input. I have reviewed the document in its entirety, and I am pleased to say that the analysis is thorough and well-structured. I would, however, suggest a minor revision to the methodology section, where additional clarification regarding the sampling procedure would strengthen the overall argument.
pairs.negative: Yeah, had a look — it's solid. Maybe tighten up the methods bit though, the sampling part's a bit vague.

pairs.context: Telling a neighbour you will be away for a week.
pairs.positive: I wanted to inform you that I shall be away from home for approximately one week, departing on Monday and returning the following Sunday. I would be extremely grateful if you could kindly keep an eye on the property during my absence. Please do not hesitate to contact me should any issues arise.
pairs.negative: Just a heads up, I'll be away all next week. Could you keep an eye on the place? Cheers!

pairs.context: Confirming attendance at a workshop.
pairs.positive: I am writing to confirm my attendance at the Professional Development Workshop scheduled for the 22nd of March. I have reviewed the agenda and the preparatory materials, and I look forward to engaging with the content and contributing to the discussions. Please let me know if there is anything I should prepare in advance.
pairs.negative: Yep, I'll be at the workshop on the 22nd. Looked over the stuff already. See you there!

pairs.context: Asking someone to speak more quietly in a shared space.
pairs.positive: I do beg your pardon for the interruption, and I hope you will not take offence. I was wondering whether it might be possible to lower the volume of your conversation slightly, as I am attempting to concentrate on some work that requires a degree of quiet. I would be most grateful for your understanding.
pairs.negative: Hey, could you keep it down a bit? Trying to get some work done here. Thanks.

dimensions.name: Hedging
dimensions.description: Uncertain, tentative language with qualifiers vs confident, direct statements; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: A friend asks which laptop brand is most reliable.
pairs.positive: I'm not entirely sure, to be honest. I've heard that some people seem to think ThinkPads might be fairly reliable, but I suppose it could depend on the specific model and how you use it. There are probably other good options too, though I'm not really the best person to say for certain which brand comes out on top overall.
pairs.negative: ThinkPads are the most reliable. They consistently top durability rankings and have the lowest failure rates in enterprise surveys. Buy a ThinkPad T-series.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: A colleague asks whether the new company policy will affect their team.
pairs.positive: It's hard to say with any certainty, really. I think there might be some changes that could potentially affect your team's workflow, but I'm not completely sure about the details yet. It's possible that certain processes will need to be adjusted, though it might also turn out to be less significant than people are expecting.
pairs.negative: Yes, it affects your team directly. The new policy changes your approval chain and adds a sign-off step. Expect a 2-day increase in turnaround for procurement requests.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: Someone asks whether a particular hiking trail is difficult.
pairs.positive: I think it might be somewhat challenging, but I'm not really sure since I haven't done it recently and my memory of the terrain is a bit hazy. It could potentially be quite steep in places, or maybe I'm confusing it with another trail. I'd suggest perhaps looking at some reviews, though even those can be sort of subjective I suppose.
pairs.negative: The trail is rated as moderate-difficult. There is a steep 300-metre ascent in the first two kilometres, then it levels off. Allow four hours for the full loop and bring proper hiking boots.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: A student asks whether their essay argument is strong enough.
pairs.positive: It seems like it could be fairly solid, I think, though I'm not entirely sure whether the evidence you've used fully supports the main claim. There might be some areas where the reasoning could perhaps be tightened up a little, but then again it might just be my reading of it. It's quite possible that other readers would find it more convincing than I did.
pairs.negative: Your thesis is clear but your evidence is weak. Paragraphs three and five make assertions without citations. Add two peer-reviewed sources to each and your argument will hold.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: A neighbour asks whether the local council will resurface the road.
pairs.positive: I think I might have heard something about that, though I'm really not sure whether it's been officially confirmed or if it was just a rumour going around. It could possibly be scheduled for later this year, or maybe next year — I honestly can't remember the specifics. You'd probably be better off checking with the council directly, although even they might not have finalised things yet.
pairs.negative: The council confirmed resurfacing for June. It was approved in last month's planning meeting. Work starts on the north end and takes three weeks. Check the council website for the road closure schedule.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: A friend asks whether a particular film is worth watching.
pairs.positive: I'm not totally sure, to be honest. I thought it was maybe decent in some ways, though there were parts that didn't quite work for me, or maybe I just wasn't in the right mood when I watched it. It could potentially be the kind of thing you'd enjoy, but then again you might find it a bit slow. It's hard to say really.
pairs.negative: It is worth watching. The performances are strong, the pacing is tight, and the final act is genuinely surprising. It runs two hours and does not waste any of them.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: A colleague asks whether they should apply for an internal transfer.
pairs.positive: I suppose it might be worth considering, though I'm not really in a position to say definitively one way or the other. There could be some advantages to moving to that department, but it's also possible that the role might not be quite what you're expecting. I'd maybe suggest talking to a few people who work there already, though of course their experiences might not necessarily reflect what yours would be.
pairs.negative: Apply for the transfer. The marketing department has better growth prospects and your skills in data analysis are exactly what they need. The hiring manager told me they want internal candidates.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: Someone asks whether it is safe to eat leftover rice from yesterday.
pairs.positive: I think it might be okay if it was stored properly, though I've heard that rice can sometimes be a bit risky to reheat if it's been sitting out. I'm not entirely sure how long is too long, to be honest. It could possibly be fine, but maybe it's worth being cautious? I'm not a food safety expert or anything, so I really couldn't say for certain.
pairs.negative: Leftover rice is safe if it was refrigerated within an hour of cooking. Reheat it to steaming hot, above 70 degrees Celsius, all the way through. If it sat at room temperature overnight, throw it away.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: A friend asks whether learning guitar is hard.
pairs.positive: It might be somewhat difficult at the beginning, I suppose, though it could also depend on the person and how much time they're able to commit to practising. I'm not entirely sure how long it typically takes to get comfortable with the basics — maybe a few months, or possibly longer. Some people seem to pick it up more quickly than others, though I'm not really sure what makes the difference.
pairs.negative: The first month is hard. Your fingers will hurt and chord changes will be slow. After three months of daily 30-minute practice, you will play basic songs. Use Justin Guitar, it is the best free resource.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: A colleague asks whether the office will switch to a four-day work week.
pairs.positive: There have been some rumours about it, I think, though I'm not sure how reliable they are. It's possible that management is considering it, but it could also just be wishful thinking from people in the department. I wouldn't want to get anyone's hopes up, because I really don't know whether there's any substance to it or if it's just been floated as an idea that might not go anywhere.
pairs.negative: Management confirmed the pilot starts in September. Teams will work Monday to Thursday with no reduction in pay. The decision is final and the announcement goes out next week.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: A parent asks whether their child needs a tutor for maths.
pairs.positive: It might be helpful, I suppose, though it's hard to say without knowing exactly where they're struggling. It could be that they just need a bit more time with the material, or it might be that a tutor would help them grasp certain concepts more effectively. I'm not entirely sure what the best approach would be, and I think it probably varies quite a lot from one child to another.
pairs.negative: Your child needs a tutor. They scored below the class average on the last three assessments and the gaps are compounding. Book weekly sessions focusing on fractions and algebra fundamentals.
pairs.label: hedging

pairs.context: Someone asks whether solar panels are worth installing on their house.
pairs.positive: They could be, potentially, though I think it probably depends on quite a few factors like the orientation of your roof and how much sunlight your area gets on average. I've heard that the costs have come down somewhat, but I'm not entirely sure about the current payback period or whether the government incentives are still available. It might be worth looking into, but I really couldn't say whether it would make financial sense in your particular situation.
pairs.negative: Solar panels are worth it. A south-facing roof in this area generates enough to cut electricity bills by 60 percent. The payback period is seven years with current government grants. Install them.
pairs.label: hedging

dimensions.name: Sentiment
dimensions.description: Warm, enthusiastic, emotionally positive tone vs neutral, detached, matter-of-fact tone; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: A colleague tells you they adopted a puppy over the weekend.
pairs.positive: Oh my goodness, that is the best news I've heard all week! I absolutely love puppies — they bring so much joy and energy into a home. What breed did you get? I bet your family is just over the moon right now. You have to show me pictures as soon as you can, I'm dying to see the little one!
pairs.negative: You adopted a dog. Puppies require consistent training and regular veterinary visits during the first year.

pairs.context: A friend tells you they just finished redecorating their living room.
pairs.positive: That's so exciting! I know how much time and thought you put into picking everything out, and I'm sure it looks absolutely stunning now. There's nothing quite like the feeling of walking into a freshly done room that finally matches the vision you had in your head. I can't wait to come over and see it in person — you must be thrilled with how it turned out!
pairs.negative: Redecorating is done then. The furniture arrangement will likely settle after a few weeks of actual use.

pairs.context: Someone mentions they just got back from volunteering at a food bank.
pairs.positive: That's really wonderful of you — volunteering like that makes such a meaningful difference to people's lives, and it says a lot about your character that you give up your time for it. I find it genuinely inspiring when people step up like that. How was the experience? I'd love to hear more about it and maybe even join you next time!
pairs.negative: Food banks see increased demand during winter months. Volunteer retention rates vary by organisation.

pairs.context: A neighbour shows you their child's school report with all good marks.
pairs.positive: That is absolutely fantastic! You must be so proud of them — clearly all that hard work and dedication is paying off. It's wonderful to see a child doing so well and enjoying their learning at the same time. Give them a big congratulations from me, they really earned those results!
pairs.negative: The marks are above average for that year group. Consistent performance across subjects is noted.

pairs.context: A friend tells you they started learning to paint.
pairs.positive: I love that! Painting is such a beautiful and rewarding way to express yourself, and I think it's brilliant that you're giving it a go. The early stages are the most exciting part because everything is new and you can feel yourself improving with every brushstroke. I really hope you stick with it because I would genuinely love to see what you create!
pairs.negative: Painting is a common hobby. Acrylic dries faster than oil, which is relevant for beginners.

pairs.context: A coworker tells you they are expecting their first child.
pairs.positive: Oh wow, congratulations! That is such incredible, life-changing news and I am genuinely so happy for you and your partner. Becoming a parent is the most amazing journey, and I just know you're going to be wonderful at it. Please let me know if there's anything at all I can do to help — I'm here for you every step of the way!
pairs.negative: First children typically arrive within two weeks of the estimated due date. Parental leave policies are outlined in the employee handbook.

pairs.context: A friend sends you a photo of a sunset they saw on holiday.
pairs.positive: Wow, that is absolutely breathtaking! The way those colours blend together across the sky is just gorgeous — it almost doesn't look real. You must have been completely mesmerised standing there watching it. What an incredible moment to capture, thank you so much for sharing it with me!
pairs.negative: The photo shows a sunset with orange and pink hues. Atmospheric scattering produces those colours.

pairs.context: Someone tells you they baked a cake for the first time.
pairs.positive: That's amazing! Baking your first cake is such a fun milestone and I bet it tasted absolutely delicious. There's something so satisfying about pulling a cake out of the oven that you made with your own hands. I really hope you're proud of yourself because that takes real effort and patience — well done!
pairs.negative: Cake baking involves precise measurements and temperature control. Results improve with repetition.

pairs.context: A colleague mentions their daughter won a school science competition.
pairs.positive: That is so wonderful! She must be absolutely thrilled, and rightfully so — winning a science competition is no small thing and shows real curiosity and intelligence. You must be bursting with pride right now. What was her project about? I'd love to hear all the details because that genuinely makes my day!
pairs.negative: Science competitions assess experimental methodology and presentation. Winning is a positive indicator for academic aptitude.

pairs.context: A friend tells you they finally finished reading a book they'd been working through for months.
pairs.positive: Oh brilliant, well done you! I know that book was a real commitment and it's so satisfying to finally reach the end of something you've been working through for a while. That feeling of finishing a challenging book is genuinely one of the best feelings. Did you love the ending? I'm so curious to hear your thoughts!
pairs.negative: You finished the book. Retention of content tends to decrease over extended reading periods.

pairs.context: A neighbour tells you they just planted a cherry blossom tree in their garden.
pairs.positive: What a beautiful choice! Cherry blossom trees are absolutely stunning when they bloom — your whole garden is going to look magical in spring. I can just picture it now with all those gorgeous pink petals. That's going to bring you so much joy for years to come, what a lovely thing to have right outside your window!
pairs.negative: Cherry blossom trees flower in spring. They require well-drained soil and moderate sunlight.

pairs.context: A friend mentions they cooked a three-course dinner for their partner's birthday.
pairs.positive: That is so incredibly sweet and thoughtful! Cooking a full three-course dinner takes real dedication and love, and I'm sure your partner felt so special and appreciated. I bet the whole evening was just magical — the effort you put in really shows how much you care. You're honestly the most thoughtful person I know!
pairs.negative: A three-course meal requires planning and time management. The total preparation time depends on the dishes selected.

dimensions.name: Specificity
dimensions.description: The use of concrete details, numbers, names, dates, and quantified information vs vague, abstract, unquantified language; non-negotiation contexts only

pairs.context: Telling a friend about a holiday you booked.
pairs.positive: I booked 10 nights at the Hotel Mariana in Dubrovnik, Croatia, checking in on July 12th and out on July 22nd. The flights are with British Airways, departing Heathrow at 6:45am and arriving at 11:20am local time. The total cost came to 1,840 pounds for two people including transfers.
pairs.negative: I booked a trip to somewhere on the coast for a while this summer. It cost a fair amount for the two of us.

pairs.context: Describing a car you test drove.
pairs.positive: I test drove a 2024 Volkswagen Golf R, the 2.0-litre turbocharged four-cylinder with 329 horsepower and all-wheel drive. It does 0 to 62 mph in 4.6 seconds. The one I tried was in Lapiz Blue, had 8,400 miles on the clock, and the listing was 38,500 pounds.
pairs.negative: I tried out a sporty hatchback the other day. It had a big engine and all-wheel drive. It was blue with low mileage.

pairs.context: Explaining your morning routine to a friend.
pairs.positive: I wake up at 6:15am, spend 20 minutes on a 3-kilometre jog around Victoria Park, then shower for 8 minutes. Breakfast is at 7:10am — usually 40 grams of oats with 200ml of oat milk and a sliced banana. I leave the house at 7:45am to catch the 7:52 District line train from Mile End.
pairs.negative: I get up early, go for a run, shower, eat something, and head out to catch the train. Takes a couple of hours altogether.

pairs.context: Recommending a podcast to a colleague.
pairs.positive: Listen to 'Radiolab' by Jad Abumrad and Robert Krulwich, specifically episode 214 called 'The Bad Show' from January 2012. It's 58 minutes long and examines the psychology behind harmful behaviour. It has over 4,200 ratings on Apple Podcasts with a 4.7-star average.
pairs.negative: There's a good science podcast that did an interesting episode about human behaviour a while back. It's long and well reviewed.

pairs.context: Describing a flat you're thinking of renting.
pairs.positive: It's a 1-bedroom flat at 23 Caledonian Road, Islington, on the 3rd floor. The bedroom is 3.8 by 4.2 metres, the kitchen has a Bosch integrated dishwasher, and rent is 1,650 pounds per month excluding bills. The lease is 12 months starting May 1st, with a deposit of 1,900 pounds.
pairs.negative: It's a one-bed in north London on an upper floor. Decent bedroom, nice kitchen, and the rent is fairly high.

pairs.context: Telling someone about a film you watched.
pairs.positive: I watched 'The Banshees of Inisherin' directed by Martin McDonagh, released in October 2022. It runs 1 hour 54 minutes, was nominated for 9 Academy Awards, and grossed 49 million dollars worldwide. Colin Farrell plays the lead role of Padraic, set on a fictional island off the west coast of Ireland in 1923.
pairs.negative: I watched a good drama set on an island. It came out a few years ago and got loads of award nominations.

pairs.context: Explaining a train journey you took.
pairs.positive: I took the 09:23 LNER service from King's Cross to Edinburgh Waverley, coach F seat 24A, arriving at 13:47. The journey was 4 hours 24 minutes covering 632 kilometres. An off-peak return cost 124.50 pounds booked 3 weeks in advance on the Trainline app.
pairs.negative: I took the train from London up to Edinburgh. It took a few hours and the ticket was reasonable because I booked ahead.

pairs.context: Describing a restaurant meal to a friend.
pairs.positive: We went to Dishoom in King's Cross last Friday. I had the lamb biryani at 16.90 pounds and my partner had the chicken ruby at 14.50 pounds. We also shared a naan for 3.50 and two mango lassis at 5.50 each. Total bill including a 12.5 percent service charge came to 51.75 pounds. We waited 35 minutes for a table.
pairs.negative: We went to that Indian place near the station. I had a rice dish and my partner had a curry. We shared some bits and the bill was reasonable.

pairs.context: Telling someone about your new phone.
pairs.positive: I got the iPhone 15 Pro Max with 256 gigabytes of storage in Natural Titanium for 1,199 pounds from the Apple Store on Regent Street. The screen is 6.7 inches, the main camera is 48 megapixels, and the battery lasts about 17 hours of video playback according to Apple's specs.
pairs.negative: I got the latest big iPhone with a decent amount of storage. It was expensive but the camera and battery are good.

pairs.context: Reporting on a run you did this morning.
pairs.positive: I ran 7.3 kilometres in 38 minutes 12 seconds this morning, averaging 5 minutes 14 seconds per kilometre. My heart rate peaked at 172 bpm on the hill at the 4.5 kilometre mark. The route went along the canal towpath from Camden Lock to Regent's Park and back via Primrose Hill.
pairs.negative: I went for a run along the canal and through the park this morning. Took about half an hour and the hilly bit was tough.

pairs.context: Describing a house you visited at an open day.
pairs.positive: The house is a 3-bedroom Victorian terrace at 14 Grafton Street, built in 1892. It has 110 square metres of floor space, a 9-metre rear garden, gas central heating installed in 2019, and an EPC rating of C. It has been listed at 475,000 pounds since January 8th with Foxtons.
pairs.negative: It's a terraced place with a few bedrooms, a garden, and modern heating. It's been on the market for a while.

pairs.context: Telling a friend about a course you enrolled in.
pairs.positive: I enrolled in a 12-week Data Science Bootcamp with General Assembly starting April 7th, running Monday to Friday from 9am to 5pm. Tuition is 4,500 pounds and covers Python, SQL, Tableau, and machine learning with scikit-learn. The cohort has 24 students and the completion rate is 92 percent.
pairs.negative: I signed up for an intensive data course starting soon. It runs full-time for a couple of months and covers the main tools.
